content.problemStatement:
During **Advitiya**, the Coding Club launched a brand-new virtual stock called **AdvitiyaCoin (ADC)** to give participants a taste of real-world trading strategies.

The price of AdvitiyaCoin changes throughout the day and is recorded in an array PPP of size NNN, where PiP_iPi​ represents the price of the stock at time index iii.

A participant can perform multiple trades during the day, following the rules below.


A **valid trade** is one of the following:

Choose indices i<ji \lt ji<j and: buy the stock at index iii and sell it at index jjj, or
Choose indices i<ji \lt ji<j and: sell the stock at index iii and buy it at index jjj.


If a trade is made by choosing indices iii and jjj, no other buy or sell operation should take place between indices iii and jjj (endpoints included).
Each trade incurs a fixed transaction cost KKK.
The profit of a trade is defined as profit=(sell price−buy price)−K\text{profit} = (\text{sell price} - \text{buy price}) - Kprofit=(sell price−buy price)−K.

A trade is considered winning if its profit is **strictly positive**.

You are given the array PPP of length NNN, denoting the prices of the stock at various time indices, as well as the transaction cost KKK.

Compute the **maximum number of winning trades** that can be performed while following all the rules.

Note that you do not need to maximize the overall profit of the trades: only the number of winning trades.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line contains two space separated integers NNN and KKK — the length of the array PPP and the transaction cost.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,…,PNP_1, \ldots, P_NP1​,…,PN​.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer on a new line: the **maximum number of winning trades** that can be performed.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The maximum number of winning trades is 222, attained as follows:

Buy at index 111, sell at index 222.
This yields a profit of 3−1−1=1>03 - 1 - 1 = 1 \gt 03−1−1=1>0, so it is winning.
Buy at index 333, sell at index 555.
This yields a profit of 4−2−1=1>04 - 2 - 1 = 1 \gt 04−2−1=1>0, so it is winning.

Test case 222: Only one winning trade is possible, for example by selling at index 111 and buying at index 444 (which is winning because it has a profit of 5−2−2=1>05-2-2 = 1 \gt 05−2−2=1>0)
It can be shown that it's impossible to have more than one winning trade while following all the given rules.
Test case 333: All prices are equal, so no profitable trade is possible. The answer is 000.
